<!-- Topic 6: Coding Exercise 2-1 -->
<!-- Slides 59-64 -->

# Coding Exercise 2-1

<!-- Slide 59 -->

## Putting Terminal I/O Together {.smaller}

+ How do `cout`, `cin`, formatting, and colors work in one small program?
+ This exercise builds a simple receipt-style report.

::: notes
Slides 59-64
:::



<!-- Slide 60 -->

---

## Program Goal

Create a program that reads one item, one quantity, and one price.

The program should print a small formatted receipt with a colored success message.

<!-- Slide 61 -->

---

## Starter Structure

```cpp
#include <iostream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <string>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    string item;
    int quantity;
    double price;

    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 62 -->

---

## Expected Interaction

```text
Item name: Notebook
Quantity: 3
Price: 4.99

Receipt
Item             Qty     Price     Total
Notebook           3      4.99     14.97
Success: receipt complete.
```

::: notes
The success line should be green when run in a terminal that supports ANSI color codes.
:::

<!-- Slide 63 -->

---

## Summary

+ Read input before calculating output.
+ Format the receipt with consistent column widths.
+ Use color only where it adds meaning.

<!-- Slide 64 -->
